An interactive fitness website that maps exercises to muscle groups — designed for beginners who are lost in a sea of contradictory information.

01 — Problem

Why does this exist?

During a PE warm-up at school, I noticed that almost my entire class was performing basic exercises incorrectly. This wasn't just my class — I saw it everywhere: parks, gyms, streets.

The problem isn't a lack of information. It's the absence of reliable, beginner-friendly guidance. Most fitness platforms overwhelm users with contradictory advice and aggressive monetisation. When beginners see no results, motivation disappears.

WHO data confirmed this isn't just a local observation — it's a global crisis. I wanted to build something practical, free, and scientifically grounded to address it.

31%
of adults worldwide are physically inactive — WHO, 2024
81%
of teenagers aged 11–17 fail to meet recommended physical activity levels
#1
barrier for beginners: confusing, contradictory, or inaccessible information
02 — Product Description

What is Muscle Training Atlas?

F — 01

Interactive Muscle Map

Click any muscle group on a front or back anatomical SVG diagram. Instantly receive level-appropriate exercises, muscle descriptions, and safety guidance — no searching required.

F — 02

Three Training Levels

Office (bodyweight only), Street (calisthenics with pull-up bars), and Gym (barbells, machines, compound lifts). No user is excluded regardless of equipment or location.

F — 03

Calorie Calculator

A personalised macro calculator built in JavaScript. Input age, sex, weight, height, and activity level to receive evidence-based daily nutrition targets for muscle growth.

F — 04

Workout Timer

Built-in countdown timer with 30s, 45s, 60s, and 90s presets. Supports structured rest periods between sets without leaving the page.

F — 05

Safety & Warm-up Guidance

Automatic warm-up reminders, responsible fitness content verified against ACE Fitness and WHO standards. No extreme or misleading advice — designed for beginners.

F — 06

Inclusive Design

Female-specific training considerations covering biomechanics, injury prevention (ACL risk), hormonal cycles, and performance differences — based on peer-reviewed research.

03 — Functional Requirements

What the product must do

Core Functionality
  • User can click any muscle on an SVG anatomical map and receive relevant exercises
  • Muscle highlights on hover and opens an exercise panel in under 0.2 seconds
  • User can switch between front and back body views seamlessly
  • User can select a training level (Office / Street / Gym) that filters all exercise content
  • Calorie calculator computes personalised macro targets based on user input
  • Workout timer counts down with start, pause, and reset functionality
Quality & Accessibility
  • Website is fully responsive across mobile, tablet, and desktop screen sizes
  • All content loads without a backend — no login, no installation required
  • Warm-up safety warning is displayed before intense exercise sections
  • Each muscle group contains at least 3 exercises per training level
  • First-time users complete core actions without any instructions or guidance
  • Website is publicly accessible via GitHub Pages at zero cost
04 — Technical Requirements

How it was built

⟨/⟩

HTML5

Full semantic structure, SVG integration, modal system, and responsive layout foundations.

#

CSS3

2100+ lines of custom CSS. Animations, media queries, hover states, and full mobile responsiveness.

JS

JavaScript

SVG click/hover logic, level filtering system, calorie calculator, countdown timer, and quote rotation.

◈

SVG Anatomy

Custom anatomical body maps vectorised in Figma and Vectorise.me. Front and back views with clickable muscle zones.

▲

Figma

Full UI/UX prototyping and design system before any code was written. Clickable prototype with flow connections.

⬡

GitHub Pages

Free public hosting. Zero backend dependency. Accessible from any device globally without installation.

✦

No Framework

Built entirely from scratch — no React, no libraries. Pure HTML, CSS, and JavaScript for maximum learning and control.

⊕

No Database

All exercise content is structured directly in JavaScript objects. Lightweight, fast, and fully offline-capable.

05 — Sketches & Process

Behind the build

The project began with anatomy research and Figma prototyping before a single line of code was written. Each stage — from SVG vectorisation to GitHub deployment — was documented as part of the IB Personal Project process.

Figma prototype — clickable flow between muscle panels
GitHub — committing SVG anatomy files to repository
VS Code — style.css at line 2107 (mobile media queries)
06 — About the Author

Who built this

AS
Designer · Developer · Researcher
Abylay Sambetbay

Grade 9 student at Nazarbayev Intellectual School, Astana, Kazakhstan. Built this project entirely solo as part of the IB MYP Personal Project. Background in judo, wrestling, and gymnastics informed the fitness content; prior experience with Python and art courses provided the creative and technical foundation. Learned Figma, HTML, CSS, and JavaScript specifically for this project — combining athletic knowledge with digital skills for the first time.

HTML / CSS / JS Figma UI/UX Design Sports Science IB MYP NIS Astana GitHub
07 — Development Timeline

How it came together

October 2025

Research & Planning

WHO data analysis, ACE Fitness content research, CARRDS source evaluation, and research plan with 10 specific questions.

October — November 2025

Design & SVG Anatomy

Figma UI/UX prototyping, front and back body vectorisation using Vectorise.me, clickable prototype with muscle flow connections.

November 2025

Core Development

HTML/CSS/JS fundamentals, SVG hover and click interaction logic, website structure and navigation built from scratch.

December 2025

Content & Level System

Exercise library across 14+ muscle groups, three-level filtering system, exercise panels with descriptions and safety notes.

January 2026

Features, Testing & Launch

Calorie calculator, workout timer, female training perspective, mobile bug fixes, user testing with supervisor, GitHub Pages deployment.

08 — Future Improvements

What comes next

v2.0

Personalised Workout Plans

User profiles that generate weekly training schedules based on goal, level, and available equipment — moving beyond single-exercise lookup.

v2.0

Progress Tracking

Local storage-based workout history so users can track sets, reps, and weights over time — showing real progress without a backend.

v2.5

Exercise Safety Notes

Short common-mistake warnings for each exercise — reducing injury risk for users training without supervision.

v2.5

Advanced Calculator

Expanded calorie calculator with fitness goal input (cut / maintain / bulk) and more granular activity levels for higher accuracy.

v3.0

Accessibility Features

High-contrast mode, keyboard navigation support, and screen reader optimisation to serve users with visual impairments.

v3.0

Multi-language Support

Kazakh and Russian translations to reach the local NIS student community and broader Central Asian audience directly.
